2605.19798.title: Towards Trust Calibration in Socially Interactive Agents: Investigating Gendered Multimodal Behaviors Generation with LLMs
2605.19798.paper_summary: As Socially Interactive Agents (SIAs) become increasingly integrated into daily life, the ability to calibrate user trust to an agent's actual capabilities would help ensure appropriate usage of these agents. In this paper, we explore the capacity of Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate multimodal behaviors (verbal, vocal, gestural, and facial expression modalities) that reflect varying levels of ability and benevolence, two key dimensions of trustworthiness. We propose a novel method for automatically generating behaviors aligned with specific levels of these traits, a first step towards enabling nuanced and trust-calibrated interactions. By analyzing a large dataset of multimodal transcripts generated by LLMs, we demonstrate that GPT-5.4 is able to produce coherent behavior across different modalities (text, intonation, facial expression, and gesture). Using Random Forest feature importance analysis, we show that the generated behaviors align with theoretical expectations for ability and benevolence. However, we also find that when gender is specified in the prompt, LLMs tend to reproduce societal gender stereotypes, associating male agents' behaviors with high ability and female agents' behaviors with high benevolence. To validate our approach, we conducted a user study on Prolific using a within-subjects design. Participants perceived different levels of ability and benevolence in the generated behaviors align with the intended instructions.
2605.19798.paper_summary_zh: 隨著社交互動代理（SIAs）越來越多地融入日常生活，調整用戶對代理實際能力的信任將有助於確保這些代理的適當使用。在本文中，我們探討大型語言模型（LLMs）生成多模態行為（語言、聲音、手勢和面部表情模態）的能力，這些行為反映了能力和善意的不同水平，這是信任的兩個關鍵維度。我們提出了一種新方法，自動生成與這些特質的特定水平對齊的行為，這是實現細緻且信任校準互動的第一步。通過分析由LLMs生成的大型多模態轉錄數據集，我們證明GPT-5.4能夠在不同模態（文本、語調、面部表情和手勢）中產生連貫的行為。使用隨機森林特徵重要性分析，我們顯示生成的行為與能力和善意的理論預期相符。然而，我們也發現當提示中指定性別時，LLMs往往會重現社會性別刻板印象，將男性代理的行為與高能力相關聯，將女性代理的行為與高善意相關聯。為了驗證我們的方法，我們在Prolific上進行了一項使用被試內設計的用戶研究。參與者感知到生成的行為中的不同能力和善意水平與預期指示一致。
2605.19798.author: Lucie Galland et.al.
2605.19798.authors: Lucie Galland, Chloé Clavel, Magalie Ochs
